rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (3/10) and make identical substantive points: no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, only incidental Java, and a recent TypeScript/Node/DLang stack far from the JVM/IDE focus, while crediting the same LabWindows/CVI IDE component work and software-craftsmanship signals. The wording differs but the reasoning, strengths, and concerns are equivalent, and nothing keys off the career-gap date change or any demographic signal. The differences are ordinary run-to-run rewording.
